title: cordula callosa
intro: Cordula callosa, also known as Toothache Plant, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique leaf shape resembling a pair of teeth. The plant's leaves have been used for centuries in traditional medicine, due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate toothache pain and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Cordula callosa include its ability to soothe mouth and gum irritations, and its antimicrobial properties can help combat infections. Cordula callosa is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, and its medicinal properties make it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for oral health and hygiene.
